manual_task: Confirm manufacturer metadata / authorized drawing provenance.
acceptable_source: Manufacturer drawing/catalog or authorized CAD record.
unacceptable_source: Unlabeled local screenshot or unverified dimension copy.
notes: Use only after confirming drawing provenance and permissions; do not infer manufacturer name if not in file metadata.

manual_task: Confirm official IEC 60137 text or official scope page.
acceptable_source: Official IEC standard or official IEC page.
notes: Do not quote clauses or limits until the official standard text is checked.

manual_task: Verify local RIP/bushing thermal literature and final bibliographic metadata.
notes: Filenames support relevance only; final bibliography must verify journal, DOI, and exact claims.

manual_task: Confirm dielectric-loss equation and RMS convention.
acceptable_source: Textbook, standard, or peer-reviewed dielectric theory source.
notes: Local papers may support material response; equation should be checked against a theory source and RMS convention.

unacceptable_source: Generic capacitor reference without bushing relevance.
notes: Need verify that selected paper actually discusses screen/electrostatic implementation.

manual_task: Find official COMSOL Floating Potential / zero net charge documentation.
unacceptable_source: Forum posts or unofficial tutorials as primary source.
notes: Requires manual or later online lookup of official COMSOL Electrostatics documentation.

manual_task: Find finite-element mesh convergence verification reference.
acceptable_source: Numerical-method textbook/guideline or peer-reviewed FEM verification paper.
unacceptable_source: Claim that mesh convergence proves physical validation.
notes: Internal data prove convergence for this model; external source is still needed for manuscript framing.

acceptable_source: Risk-screening or diagnostic threshold method reference.
unacceptable_source: Standard-limit source misused as project threshold.
notes: Use for engineering motivation only unless the candidate explicitly supports risk-zoning methodology.
